requirement: Users can create invoices with required fields such as supplier name, invoice number, invoice date, payment term, currency, control total, and chart of accounts.
Users can view and manage invoice status, including approved, reviewing, and draft.
Users can attach documents and images to invoices.
Users can view and manage invoice history, including revision history and document reviewers.
Users can submit invoices for approval and track their status.
Users can view and manage invoice reports, including invoice control total and payment terms.
Users can configure and manage invoice tolerances and blockers.
Users can view and manage on-hold invoices.
Users can edit invoices in a sequence and manage invoice matching.
Users can view and manage credit notes and void approved invoices.
Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Supplier can create an invoice via Coupa Supplier Portal, SAN/PO Link, AP Mailbox/Invoice Smash, or Direct Entry.
Supplier can create a PO-backed invoice by flipping a relevant PO into an invoice in Coupa Supplier Portal or SAN.
Coupa performs auto-matching of PO, GRN, and Invoice for 3-way matching.

Supplier can add various invoice fields such as Line Number, Line Type, Description, Quantity, UOM, Unit Price, Total Price, PO Line, Contract, Supplier Part Number, Commodity, Period, Billing String, Tax, WHT Applicable, Car Plate ID, and Inspection Code.
AP Team can create a purchase order-backed invoice in Coupa.
Supplier can create a non-PO backed invoice in Coupa.
AP Team can review and update invoice details in Coupa.
Requester can approve invoices in Coupa.
Financial Manager can approve invoices in Coupa if required.
AP Team can reject invoices in Coupa and dispute with suppliers.
AP Team can rectify invoices in Coupa and resubmit for approval.
Coupa can check for VAT and other charges before approval.
NetSuite can process payments and send updates to Coupa.
Coupa can update the final payment status as 'Paid'.
Requester can create a non-PO invoice through the invoice form, add the invoice category, and submit it in draft status in Coupa.
Accounts Payable can review, update, and submit the invoice for approval after it is picked from the draft state.
Requester can review and approve the invoice to confirm the legitimacy of the spend.
Final AP Clerk can perform a final check and approval before marking the invoice as ready for payment.
Approved invoices can be processed for payment and updated in Coupa.
Reports can be extracted per invoice category to manage the spend.
Supplier can create a credit note by flipping an invoice to credit note in the Purchase Order section.
AP Team can review, reject, or approve the credit note after it is submitted.
AP Team can create a credit note directly in Coupa for PO-backed credit notes.
AP Team can void an approved invoice by clicking the red 'u-turn' icon and confirming the action.
AP Team can bulk load invoices in Coupa by downloading a template, filling in the required details, and uploading the template.
Supplier can submit invoices to Coupa for approval and payment.
Invoice status can be updated through various stages including Draft, Processing, AP Hold, Pending Action, Pending Receipt, On Hold, Pending Approval, Booking Hold, Approved, Disputed, and Rejected.
Invoice can be disputed and sent to the supplier for corrections, and can be withdrawn from dispute or resolved by the supplier.
Invoice can be approved and marked for payment after passing additional checks for correct syncing into the ERP and matching validations.
NetSuite processes the payment and sends the update to Coupa.
Final payment status is reflected in Coupa.
Buyer can create a standard invoice for approved suppliers.
Invoice can be submitted for approval after passing validation checks.
Invoice can be placed on hold for additional review and approval.
Invoice can be matched against purchase orders and receipts for accurate invoicing.
Invoice can be automatically approved if 2-way or 3-way matching is satisfied.
Invoice can be disputed to the supplier for corrections.
Invoice can be voided by an AP user or Admin role.
Invoice can be adjusted to apply available balances to the invoice net balance.
AP user can select and edit multiple invoices in a sequence without having to exit the current screen.
AP user can move to the next invoice or credit note after saving or submitting an invoice.
Coupa runs invoice tolerances to determine whether invoices can be automatically approved or require further review and approval.
Tolerances for Malaysia will be set at 8% SST variance, while other entities will have tolerances set as per specified rules.
An invoice goes on 'Pending Receipt' or 'On hold' status if it does not satisfy matching or invoice tolerance rules.
Coupa provides two types of invoice tolerance settings: Chart of Accounts-level tolerances and Commodity-level tolerances.
Commodity-level tolerances override Chart of Accounts-level tolerances and apply to all Charts of Accounts.
System blocks invoice submission if it doesn't meet admin-set criteria, or displays a soft warning message.
Coupa provides various reporting levels, including standard, advanced, and dashboards, with features like filtering, custom views, and scheduled reports.
System compares manually entered invoice total to calculated total and displays an error if they differ.
System has various roles with different access levels, including admin, user, buyer, accounts payable, accounting supervisor, edit requisition as approver, and edit invoice as approver.
Payment information is integrated from NetSuite to Coupa.
Configure invoicing tolerance checks with a '0' value tolerance.
Enable group reporting in USD currency.
Enable central receiving role for exception cases.
